questions.question_text: In New York, which license category allows a person to operate their own real estate firm and supervise agents?
distractors: Associate broker
Real estate salesperson
Licensed real estate assistant
questions.explanation: Only a licensed real estate broker may operate a firm and supervise salespersons and associate brokers. Associate brokers hold a broker license but work under another broker rather than running their own office.

questions.question_text: A broker opens a branch office 40 miles from the main office and assigns a salesperson to manage it. What does DOS Regulation 175.21 require?
questions.correct_answer: The broker must provide adequate supervision
distractors: A separate broker license is required for each branch
An associate broker must manage the branch office
The branch must be within 25 miles of the main office
questions.explanation: DOS Regulation 175.21 requires brokers to provide adequate supervision of all offices and licensees. There is no distance restriction, but the broker remains vicariously liable for the acts of supervised agents at every location.

questions.question_text: Under New York Real Property Law, a broker is held vicariously liable when a salesperson makes a misrepresentation during a listing presentation. Why?
questions.correct_answer: The broker is responsible for supervised licensees' acts
distractors: The broker personally approved the misrepresentation
Salespersons are independent contractors under RPL
Vicarious liability applies only to associate brokers
questions.explanation: Under vicarious liability, the supervising broker bears legal responsibility for the professional acts and omissions of all licensees working under them. This applies regardless of whether the broker had direct knowledge of the misconduct.

questions.question_text: What document must a New York licensee carry as proof of active license status?
questions.correct_answer: Pocket card issued by the DOS
distractors: Original license certificate
Broker's letter of sponsorship
Copy of the exam score report
questions.explanation: The DOS issues a pocket card to each licensed salesperson, associate broker, and broker. This card serves as portable proof of licensure and must be carried when conducting real estate activities.

questions.question_text: DOS Regulation 175.23 requires brokers to maintain transaction records for a specified period. A broker destroys files after one year. What risk does the broker face?
questions.correct_answer: Disciplinary action for violating retention rules
distractors: No risk because one year exceeds the minimum period
A fine only if a consumer files a formal complaint
Automatic license revocation without a hearing
questions.explanation: Regulation 175.23 requires brokers to maintain records of all transactions for at least three years. Destroying records after one year violates this regulation and subjects the broker to disciplinary proceedings by the DOS.

questions.question_text: A salesperson deposits a client's earnest money into their personal checking account. Which violation has occurred?
distractors: Unauthorized practice of law
Operating without a license
Blind advertising
questions.explanation: Commingling occurs when a licensee mixes client funds with personal or business operating funds. Client earnest money must be deposited into the broker's designated escrow or trust account, never a personal account.

questions.question_text: A real estate ad says 'Beautiful 3BR near downtown' with no broker identification. This is an example of which violation?
distractors: Commingling of funds
Steering
Misrepresentation of square footage
questions.explanation: Blind advertising occurs when a licensee places an ad without identifying the brokerage firm. New York law requires all real estate advertisements to clearly include the name of the licensed broker to ensure transparency.

questions.question_text: A broker's license is revoked by the DOS. Under current New York rules effective late 2025, how long must the broker wait before reapplying?
questions.correct_answer: Three years from the date of revocation
distractors: One year from the date of revocation
Five years from the date of revocation
The broker may reapply immediately after completing CE
questions.explanation: Effective late 2025, New York requires a three-year waiting period after license revocation before the individual may apply for a new license. This extended period reflects the seriousness of conduct warranting revocation.

questions.question_text: A person collects referral fees for directing buyers to a specific brokerage but holds no real estate license. Which violations apply?
questions.correct_answer: Operating without a license and receiving kickbacks
distractors: Blind advertising and commingling of client funds
Misrepresentation and unlawful steering practices
Only the brokerage is liable for the fee arrangement
questions.explanation: Accepting compensation for real estate referrals without a license constitutes operating without a license. The fee itself is an illegal kickback. Both the unlicensed person and the brokerage paying the fee face penalties under RPL.

questions.question_text: Under New York Real Property Law, practicing as a real estate broker or salesperson without a license is classified as which type of offense?
distractors: A felony
A civil infraction only
A violation with no criminal record
questions.explanation: Operating without a real estate license is a misdemeanor under New York Real Property Law. This criminal classification means the offender can face fines and potential jail time in addition to any DOS administrative penalties.

questions.question_text: A broker's unlicensed assistant answers the phone and schedules showing appointments for buyers. Has the assistant exceeded permissible duties?
questions.correct_answer: No, scheduling is an allowed administrative task
distractors: Yes, only licensed agents may schedule showings
Yes, any direct buyer contact requires a license
No, assistants may also negotiate contract offers
questions.explanation: Unlicensed assistants may perform administrative tasks such as answering phones and scheduling appointments. They may not show properties, discuss pricing, or negotiate terms, as those activities require a real estate license.

questions.question_text: An unlicensed assistant conducts a showing and discusses the list price with a prospective buyer. Which statement is correct?
questions.correct_answer: The assistant performed a licensed activity illegally
distractors: This is permitted if the broker authorized it
Discussing price is allowed but negotiating is not
Showing property is considered an administrative task
questions.explanation: Showing property and discussing pricing are licensed activities under New York law. An unlicensed assistant who performs these tasks is engaging in the unauthorized practice of real estate, which exposes both the assistant and the supervising broker to penalties.

questions.question_text: An attorney negotiates the sale of a client's property and collects a commission. Does this require a real estate license in New York?
questions.correct_answer: No, attorneys are exempt from licensing requirements
distractors: Yes, all commission earners must be licensed
Only if the attorney is not admitted to the NY bar
Only if the property value exceeds $500,000
questions.explanation: New York law exempts licensed attorneys from real estate licensing requirements when performing real estate activities within the scope of their legal practice. This exemption recognizes the overlap between legal services and real estate transactions.

questions.question_text: Which individual is exempt from obtaining a New York real estate license to sell property?
questions.correct_answer: An owner selling their own property
distractors: A part-time referral agent working weekends
A property manager collecting rent for others
A mortgage broker marketing listings online
questions.explanation: Property owners selling their own real estate are exempt from licensing because they are acting on their own behalf, not representing others for compensation. Referral agents, property managers acting for others, and mortgage brokers marketing listings all require licensure.

questions.question_text: A court appoints an individual to sell real property from a decedent's estate. Must this person hold a real estate license?
distractors: Yes, unless the estate value is under $100,000
Yes, all property sales require a licensed agent
Only if the sale involves a commercial property
questions.explanation: Individuals appointed by a court, such as executors, administrators, or receivers, are exempt from real estate licensing requirements when selling property as part of their court-ordered duties. This exemption ensures estate administration proceeds without licensing barriers.
